constraint.reason: Backend code targets Python 3.11; 3.12 may introduce deprecation breaks
API stability; avoid 0.116+ breaking changes until verified
SQLModel API is unstable; pin to current minor
Frontend code depends on React 18; migration to 19 not planned

tool.name: vpn-tools
tool.value: awg, awg-quick, iptables, ipset, ip, ping

module-level.focus: Fast deterministic unit and contract checks for single modules

wave-level.focus: Checks all merged surfaces touched by a wave; integration-level without external services

phase-level.focus: Broader integration and regression confidence; full test suite including slow tests

Testing.mocking-policy: Prefer fakes and narrow stubs over broad monkey-patching. Mock external services (YooKassa, Telegram API, DNS resolvers) at the boundary, not at the implementation level.

Observability.redaction: Never log secrets, credentials, tokens, private keys, or raw high-risk payloads. All sensitive data must be redacted before logging.

log-levels.info: Normal operation (user login, config generation, payment received)
log-levels.warning: Degraded but functional (DNS timeout, retry scheduled, stale binding detected)
log-levels.error: Functional failure (DB connection lost, webhook processing failed, VPN config generation error)
log-levels.critical: Security or data integrity incident (secret exposure detected, unauthorized admin access, schema corruption)

DeliveryShape.shape: Two-node Docker Compose deployment: RU entry node + DE exit node. Nginx reverse proxy, PostgreSQL primary database, Redis cache. Backend is containerized; VPN routing and host tools (awg, iptables, ipset) run on host with container mounts for config directories.
DeliveryShape.deployment: install.sh provisions host, docker-compose.yml orchestrates services, deploy/ scripts handle remote node setup
DeliveryShape.constraints: VPN and routing code is host-coupled — not purely containerized. Deployment security depends on manual secret management (no vault integration yet).
